rationale: Both records represent the same real-world entity: Hudson Yards Development Corporation. All strong identity fields agree — identical entity_name ('Hudson Yards Development Corporation') and identical entity_type ('development corporation'). The record-ID differences are purely source-local naming conventions: one uses abbreviated 'dev-corp' with a date suffix '-2012', the other spells out 'development-corp'. Both share the alias 'entity-hudson-yards-development-corporation' and the validator confirms them as duplicates via shared identity key. Under the Same-object test (test 2): strong identity fields agree, and the id/name differences are explained entirely by source-local abbreviation and date-suffix conventions. Under per-kind rules: acronym + expanded name of same org DO merge. Decision: merge.
